name: PipelineWise Open-Source ELT Framework
what_it_does: Open-source Singer-based ELT framework — enabling data teams to run configurable batch ELT pipelines from 20+ sources (MySQL, PostgreSQL, Kafka, S3, Salesforce, Snowflake) to destinations (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, PostgreSQL) using YAML-based configuration, with log-based CDC for incremental sync, built and open-sourced by Wise (formerly TransferWise).
use_cases: Running Singer tap/target pipelines via PipelineWise for open-source ELT without managed SaaS costs
Configuring MySQL binlog CDC via PipelineWise for incremental database replication
Syncing PostgreSQL via PipelineWise logical replication for incremental data loading
Orchestrating ELT pipelines via PipelineWise CLI for automated data warehouse population
Building custom Singer taps via PipelineWise SDK for organization-specific data sources
not_for: Teams needing managed SaaS (PipelineWise is self-hosted only)
Real-time streaming (PipelineWise is scheduled batch ELT)
Non-technical teams (PipelineWise requires Python and YAML expertise)
best_when: An engineering team wants a self-hosted, open-source ELT framework using the Singer spec — avoiding Airbyte/Fivetran costs while leveraging Wise's production-tested Singer connectors.
avoid_when: You need a managed SaaS ELT tool, real-time CDC streaming, or lack engineering resources to operate self-hosted infrastructure.

auth.notes: NO REST API — PipelineWise is a CLI tool with YAML configuration. CLI: pipelinewise import, pipelinewise run-tap, pipelinewise status. YAML config: connection settings and credentials per tap/target. Per-connector auth: each Singer tap uses its own auth (MySQL user/password, PostgreSQL DSN, Salesforce OAuth2, GitHub token, etc.). Self-hosted only: run on your own infrastructure (Docker, VMs, K8s). Python: requires Python 3.x environment. Singer protocol: tap-to-target message passing over stdin/stdout.

pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers; Wise offers no commercial support
pricing.notes: UK company (Wise/TransferWise, London). Open-sourced by Wise engineering team. Apache 2.0 license. Singer spec: open standard for data connectors (taps = sources, targets = destinations). 20+ built-in taps: MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, MongoDB, Kafka, Snowflake, Salesforce, Zendesk, GitHub, Jira, S3, etc. Targets: Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, PostgreSQL, S3. CDC support: MySQL binlog, PostgreSQL logical replication for incremental sync. State management: bookmarks for incremental sync. YAML-first: all configuration in YAML files — GitOps-friendly. Docker: official Docker images for containerized deployment. Note: Wise uses PipelineWise internally for their own data warehouse — production battle-tested.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: NO REST API — CLI tool with YAML config. Singer protocol (taps + targets). Per-connector auth. Self-hosted Python. Apache 2.0. UK/Wise origin. CDC via MySQL binlog or PostgreSQL logical replication. Docker support.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Singer state management: bookmark-based incremental sync is idempotent. Full sync mode: idempotent by design. CDC mode: log sequence number tracking ensures no duplicates. State file: persisted per tap run for resumable sync.
known_agent_gotchas: NO REST API: PipelineWise is a CLI tool — automate via shell scripts, cron, or Airflow operators
Singer protocol: understand tap/target pattern — taps emit JSON records, targets consume them via stdin/stdout pipes
YAML configuration: import config with 'pipelinewise import --dir /path/to/config' before running taps
CDC prerequisites: MySQL requires binlog_format=ROW; PostgreSQL requires wal_level=logical and replication slot
Python environment: requires Python 3.x with pip-installed PipelineWise and individual tap/target packages
Maintenance status: Wise actively maintains but community PRs may be slow — check GitHub for recent activity

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS via source/destination infrastructure (operator responsibility). Apache 2.0: auditable codebase. Self-hosted: all credentials stay in your environment. YAML config: store credentials via environment variables, not plaintext YAML (use env var substitution). Per-connector auth: use least-privilege credentials for each source. Wise-tested: production use at Wise provides some security assurance. No central secret storage — manage securely per deployment.
